title: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Diet and Mental Health

A

The idea that food can influence the mind is not new. Families have long noticed that children become difficult when they skip breakfast, and many adults recognise the low mood that follows several days of poor sleep and hurried meals. For much of the twentieth century, however, mental health was usually discussed in terms of personality, family experience or medical treatment, while diet was treated mainly as a matter of weight and physical disease. That separation is now being questioned. Psychiatrists, nutrition scientists and public-health researchers are investigating whether everyday eating patterns can affect mood, concentration and emotional resilience.

B

A common misunderstanding is that diet and mental health are linked only through willpower. If someone feels low and eats badly, the simple explanation is that they lack discipline; if they eat better, they are assumed to have stronger character. Researchers argue that this view is too narrow. Food choices are shaped by income, work schedules, advertising, stress and the availability of fresh ingredients. A nurse finishing a night shift, a student revising for examinations and a parent cooking after a long commute may all make choices that look irrational from the outside but are understandable within their circumstances. Blaming the individual can therefore hide the pressures that make healthy eating difficult.

C

Scientific interest has grown partly because the biological pathways are becoming clearer. Meals affect blood glucose, inflammation and the gut microbiome, the community of bacteria and other organisms living in the digestive system. These organisms help produce chemical signals that communicate with the brain through nerves, hormones and the immune system. Studies comparing people with different diets have found associations between varied, fibre-rich meals and lower rates of depressive symptoms. Such studies do not prove that one meal causes one mood, but they do suggest that mental health is connected to systems far beyond the brain alone.

D

The quality of evidence is uneven. Some trials show that people encouraged to eat more vegetables, legumes, whole grains, fish and nuts report fewer symptoms of depression after several months. Participants often describe more stable energy during the day, especially when highly processed snacks are replaced by regular meals. Yet researchers warn against exaggerated claims. A diet cannot replace therapy, medication or social support for people with serious illness. Nor is there a single menu that protects everyone. Culture, allergies, cooking skills, income and personal preference all influence whether a nutritional plan can be followed for long enough to matter.

E

Stress complicates the picture further. When people are anxious, tired or under time pressure, they may turn to sweet or fatty foods because these offer immediate comfort. The effect is usually temporary. A sugary snack may reduce tension for a short period, but it can be followed by a slump in energy and greater irritability. This pattern is common during demanding periods such as examinations, caring responsibilities or insecure employment. It also helps explain why advice that sounds simple in a clinic may fail in ordinary life. People do not eat in laboratories; they eat while managing money, deadlines, family expectations and emotion.

F

For this reason, many specialists now recommend modest changes rather than strict food rules. One approach is to alter the food environment: keeping fruit, yoghurt or nuts where they are easy to reach, and making highly processed snacks less automatic rather than forbidden. Another is to plan regular meals before stressful periods begin, so that hunger does not make decisions on behalf of the eater. Cooking with other people, sharing meals and learning a small number of reliable recipes can also reduce isolation and make change feel less like self-punishment. The emerging message is cautious but useful: diet is not a cure for mental illness, but it is one part of the conditions in which mental health is protected or undermined.

Questions 14-16
Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.

Which paragraph contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 14-16 on your answer sheet.

NB You may use any letter more than once.

14 biological evidence of a link between diet and mood

15 mention of false assumptions people poor may eat badly

16 reference to real situations eating become hard follow

Questions 17-22

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 17-22 on your answer sheet.

What connects diet and mental health?

Many people think that poor eating habits are mainly the result of weak 17 .................... . Others believe that better diet simply shows stronger character. But researchers say this ignores the wider pressures that shape food choices. Scientific studies suggest that diet may influence mental health through several body systems. For example, organisms in the digestive system can produce chemical 18 .................... that communicate with the brain.

Some studies connect varied, fibre-rich meals with lower levels of depressive 19 .................... . During stressful times, such as preparing for 20 ...................., people may choose sweet or fatty foods because they provide immediate 21 .................... . However, this effect does not usually last and may be followed by greater 22 .................... .

Questions 23 and 24

Choose TWO letters, A-E.

Write the correct letters in boxes 23 and 24 on your answer sheet.

Which TWO comparisons between people who improve their diet and those who do not are mentioned in the text?

A They may report fewer symptoms of depression.
B They always need less medical treatment.
C Their energy may become more stable during the day.
D They usually spend less money on food.
E Their family relationships always improve.

Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

Write the correct letters in boxes 25 and 26 on your answer sheet.

Which TWO recommendations for making dietary change easier does the writer give?

A changing the food environment so healthier options are easier to reach
B banning all sweet and fatty foods from the home
C following one strict menu designed for everyone
D planning regular meals before stressful periods begin
E avoiding meals with other people until eating habits improve
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: biological evidence of a link between diet and mood

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: Meals affect blood glucose, inflammation and the gut microbiome

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะย่อหน้า C ให้ biological pathways เช่น blood glucose, inflammation และ gut microbiome ที่เชื่อม diet กับ mood/mental health

Paraphrased Vocabulary: biological evidence = biological pathways; diet = meals; mood = depressive symptoms / mental health

Question 15: mention of false assumptions people poor may eat badly

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: A common misunderstanding is that diet and mental health are linked only through willpower.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะ false assumptions = common misunderstanding และย่อหน้า B อธิบายความเข้าใจผิดว่าคนกินไม่ดีเพราะ lack discipline หรือ willpower เท่านั้น ทั้งที่จริงมีปัจจัยรายได้ งาน ความเครียด และการเข้าถึงอาหารเข้ามาเกี่ยวข้อง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: poor may eat badly = common misunderstanding diet mental health linked through willpower; answer signal = B



Question 16: reference to real situations eating become hard follow

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: advice that sounds simple in a clinic may fail in ordinary life

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E เพราะย่อหน้า E อธิบายว่า advice ในคลินิกอาจทำจริงยากในชีวิตจริง เมื่อคนต้องจัดการ money, deadlines, family expectations และ emotion

Paraphrased Vocabulary: eating become hard follow = advice sounds simple clinic may fail ordinary life; answer signal = E

Correct Answer: willpower

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: diet and mental health are linked only through willpower

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ willpower เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary paraphrase จาก passage ว่า poor eating habits ถูกมองว่าเกิดจาก willpower เท่านั้น


Paraphrased Vocabulary: eating habits and result of = diet and mental health are linked only through willpower; answer signal = willpower

Question 24: Which TWO comparisons between people who improve their diet and those who do not are mentioned in the text?

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: A|C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: Participants often describe more stable energy during the day, especially when highly processed snacks are replaced by regular meals.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบอีกข้อคือ C เพราะ passage บอกว่าผู้เข้าร่วมมี more stable energy during the day เมื่อเปลี่ยน processed snacks เป็น regular meals

Paraphrased Vocabulary: energy may become more stable = more stable energy; improve their diet = snacks are replaced by regular meals

Question 25: Which TWO recommendations for making dietary change easier does the writer give?

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A|D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: One approach is to alter the food environment: keeping fruit, yoghurt or nuts where they are easy to reach

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบข้อหนึ่งคือ A เพราะ writer แนะนำ alter the food environment โดยวาง fruit, yoghurt หรือ nuts ในที่หยิบง่าย

Paraphrased Vocabulary: changing = alter; healthier options = fruit, yoghurt or nuts; easier to reach = easy to reach

Question 26: Which TWO recommendations for making dietary change easier does the writer give?

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: A|D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Jan 2026 Passage 2 - Diet and Mental Health

Exact Portion: plan regular meals before stressful periods begin

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบอีกข้อคือ D เพราะ passage แนะนำให้ plan regular meals before stressful periods begin เพื่อไม่ให้ hunger ตัดสินใจแทนเรา

Paraphrased Vocabulary: planning = plan; regular meals = regular meals; before stressful periods begin = before stressful periods begin
